Preferred_Parent.Parent_String: lighting devices by form [300179870], lighting devices [300037581], lighting equipment [300428218], furnishings by form or function [300037342], furnishings (works) [300037336], Furnishings (hierarchy name) [300037335], Furnishings and Equipment (hierarchy name) [300264551], Objects Facet [300264092]

Non-Preferred_Parent.Parent_String: lighting devices by location or context [300180213], lighting devices [300037581], lighting equipment [300428218], furnishings by form or function [300037342], furnishings (works) [300037336], Furnishings (hierarchy name) [300037335], Furnishings and Equipment (hierarchy name) [300264551], Objects Facet [300264092]

slide fixtures [300182413], ceiling fixtures (lighting fixtures) [300181215], lighting fixtures by location or context [300180088], lighting fixtures [300180081], lighting devices by form [300179870], lighting devices [300037581], lighting equipment [300428218], furnishings by form or function [300037342], furnishings (works) [300037336], Furnishings (hierarchy name) [300037335], Furnishings and Equipment (hierarchy name) [300264551], Objects Facet [300264092]

Descriptive_Note.Note_Text: Lighting devices designed to hang from the roof or ceiling having two or more branches, holding candles, burners, or lamps; often ornamental.

Artefactos de iluminación diseñados para ser colgados desde un techo o cielo raso que tienen dos o más brazos, generalmente curvados, que tienen velas, quemadores bombillas; frecuentemente ornamentales.
Plafondverlichting met meestal twee of meer omhoog krullende armen, waaraan kaarsen, branders of lampen.
由屋頂或天花板懸掛下來的照明設備，具有兩個以上的分枝懸臂，通常往上捲翹，可固定蠟燭、燃燒器或燈，通常帶有裝飾性。
Beleuchtungsvorrichtung, die von der Decke hängt und zwei oder mehr Arme hat, die Kerzen, Brenner oder Lampen halten; häufig verziert.

Term_Source.Page: lighting devices which hang from the ceiling and provide sockets for many candles

Source.Source_ID: 2000011141/Fleming and Honour, Dictionary of the Decorative Arts (1977)
2000012291/Downs and Du Pont, American furniture: Queen Anne and Chippendale (1952)

Term_Source.Page: Anne Serio, Curator, National Museum of American History, 4/90
